kpis.title: False-green build rate
kpis.friction: Builds that report exit 0 without verified compiled output poison downstream work with silent failures the harness then pays to re-diagnose.
kpis.notes: Computable today from ~/.claude/state/build-queue/results/<seq>.json (hygiene.build_fidelity in {log-failure-override, no-output} over non-n/a build records). Baseline capture is workstation-only (--capture-baseline; retro-derivable from results history where retained) — this registry ships provenance-honest pending, never a fabricated zero. Renders NO-DATA on machines without build-queue state.

kpis.title: Queue wait time p50
kpis.friction: Serialized Cognito builds queue behind each other; unmeasured wait time hides whether the FIFO serializer is still cheaper than the poisoned-artifact failures it prevents.
kpis.notes: NOT computable yet (verified 2026-07-04): results/<seq>.json records seq/exit_code/ended_at/hygiene but no queued-at/started-at pair (started_at exists only in the transient active.lock). Workstation-deferred follow-up: build-queue-runner.ps1/build-queue.ps1 persist queued_at + started_at onto results/<seq>.json (no .ps1 modified from this lane per operator direction). Selector renders NO-DATA with a footnote until then.

kpis.title: Raw-invocation deny recurrence
kpis.friction: Agents repeatedly attempting raw dotnet/nx builds in Cognito worktrees burn a hook deny plus a redirect turn each time; recurrence measures whether the skill-routing lesson is sticking.
kpis.notes: build-queue-enforce.sh denies are not ledgered today (verified 2026-07-04). Workstation-deferred follow-up: a best-effort, fail-OPEN-preserving hook-side append of build-queue denies into the deny ledger (hook untouched from this lane per operator direction). Selector counts deny-ledger entries whose reason_head carries the build-queue signature; an absent ledger renders NO-DATA.

kpis.title: Runaway containment trip rate
kpis.friction: Every containment-refusal is a runaway a cycle subagent attempted (routing/lifecycle/recursive-dispatch ops); the trip rate measures how often the containment layer is actually load-bearing versus dead weight.
kpis.notes: Bound to the harness-telemetry-ledger D4-B 'containment-refusal' event (exit-3 refusal sites). The ledger landed 2026-07-04 with no accumulated history yet — provenance pending until a real run window exists (--capture-baseline on the workstation).

kpis.title: Halt dwell time p50
kpis.friction: A halted item (BLOCKED/NEEDS_INPUT and kin) accrues wall-clock dwell until its sentinel resolves; dwell measures how quickly the operator loop turns halts back into forward progress.
kpis.notes: Bound to the D4-B 'halt' event (dispatch terminal_reason in the halt set) paired with the next same-item 'sentinel-resolved' event; computed by pipeline_visualizer.trends.halt_dwell (median of resolved dwells; unresolved halts are honest open halts, never counted as zero). Supersedes the date-granularity *_RESOLVED_<date> sentinel-scan fallback the SPEC computability table anticipated.

kpis.title: Cycles per completion
kpis.friction: Forward cycles spent per __mark_complete__/__mark_fixed__ terminal measure orchestration overhead — corrective cycles, gate refusals, and loop churn all inflate it.
kpis.notes: Bound to D4-B 'cycle-begin' vs 'pseudo-applied' events whose data.pseudo is a terminal (__mark_complete__/__mark_fixed__); computed by pipeline_visualizer.trends.cycles_per_completion (None when the window has zero completions — never a fabricated ratio). Marker counters alone could not supply this (deleted at --run-end), which is why the row waited for the ledger.

kpis.title: Canary trip precision
kpis.friction: A canary that trips on noise trains the operator to ignore it; every false trip costs a triaged bug stub and erodes trust in the tripwire. Trip precision measures whether the hair-triggered bands are catching real regressions versus crying wolf.
kpis.notes: Precision = trips whose canary-revert-<id> item was NOT closed-as-noise, over all trips in the window. Signal selector registered in kpi-scorecard.py _SOURCES at spec-finalization (renders NO-DATA until compute wired); computation + this registry row land in the feature's Phase 4. Baseline is unmeasurable until the canary has tripped >=5 times — provenance stays 'pending' (honest, never a fabricated zero) and the band is set with --capture-baseline once real trip data exists.

kpis.title: Failed Reads / dangling references on .claude/skill-config/ paths
kpis.friction: The #1 tool-error cluster in the mined AlgoBooth corpus: 377 failed Reads of .claude/skill-config/commit-policy.md across ~100 sessions (>10% of all tool errors) — referenced 29x across 17 skill files, present in cognito-forms but absent in algobooth, with no way to declare intended-absent; plus dead prose pointers (long-build-ownership.md) and schema-unvalidated load-bearing JSON (build-queue-ops.json) invisible to lint-skills.py.
kpis.notes: Manual evidence of record: 377 failed commit-policy.md Reads across ~100 mined AlgoBooth sessions (proposal session 2026-07-11, transcript mining) — a corpus total, not a windowed rate, hence provenance pending. The registered machine signal (deny-ledger process-friction-count) is the coarse channel: incident-scan clusters recurring tool-error friction into the ledger as process-friction entries. The Phase 0 quick win (repos/algobooth/.claude/skill-config/commit-policy.md, authored this feature) should drive the commit-policy component of this signal to ~0 immediately; the MANIFEST.json + lint-skill-config.py sweep (Phases 1-2, also this feature) prevent new clusters of the same class from forming. DEFERRED (documented, not blocking): a dedicated session-log-mining selector (e.g. skill-config-failed-read-count over the session-log corpus, mirroring skill-usage-miner.py's corpus walk) would give a precise windowed count instead of this coarse proxy — implementation SHOULD register that selector and re-point this row, then run --capture-baseline, following the same registry-follow-up pattern as build-queue-wait-time-p50 in this same registry.

kpis.title: Conclusive efficacy verdicts produced
kpis.friction: An intervention ledger that never yields a CONFIRMED/REFUTED verdict is measurement theater; every ungraded record is a harness change whose effect is assumed, not observed. Verified 2026-07-11: zero verdicts across 25 records after 22 hardening rounds.
kpis.notes: Counts ## Review sections whose verdict is CONFIRMED or REFUTED. Baseline pending until the split-brain fix lands and the first reviews come due. Registered at efficacy-signal-integrity finalization (kpi-scorecard.py _SOURCES['intervention-records']); compute reads docs/interventions/*.md ## Review <date> sections directly (a pure-read scan, never a re-implementation of efficacy-eval.py's own verdict arithmetic).

kpis.title: Confounded-verdict ratio
kpis.friction: When co-shipped interventions share one target signal, the D6 cap turns every due review into INCONCLUSIVE (confounded) — attribution is impossible by construction, not by data. Verified 2026-07-11: 6 of 8 measurable records share event:gate-refusal with overlapping windows.
kpis.notes: Share of due reviews capped INCONCLUSIVE (confounded) over all due reviews in the window. The sub-signal seam (efficacy-signal-integrity Phase 1 — event:<type>/<signature> targets) is the lever that moves it down.

kpis.title: Canary closure latency p50
kpis.friction: A canary that cannot close is dead weight that will eventually mass-expire as closed-clean (no-data), laundering unwatched changes as observed. Verified 2026-07-11: 19 canaries open, zero ever closed or tripped.
kpis.notes: Median opened-to-closed days over canaries closed in the window, EXCLUDING closed-clean (no-data) ceiling closes (those are counted separately by the canary-health alarm's projected_no_data_close_count — a no-data close must never improve this KPI).

kpis.title: MCP-validation round trips per feature
kpis.friction: Step-9 MCP validation peeling one defect seam per full pipeline loop (mcp-validation-peels-one-seam-per-loop) burned repeated BLOCKED->add-phase round trips per feature before the seam-batching fix landed; this KPI is the deferred follow-up that would prove the fix actually reduced round trips instead of only reasoning about it from the fixed prose.
kpis.notes: Registered per mcp-validation-peels-one-seam-per-loop's Deferred Follow-Up item 1 (docs/bugs/_archive/mcp-validation-peels-one-seam-per-loop/PHASES.md) and picked up by stale-runtime-health-200-false-blocked's STATE-lane close-out. Signal selector NOT YET wired in kpi-scorecard.py's _SOURCES enum as of this row's addition (that file was owned by a concurrent agent when this row was written) -- --lint will report an unregistered-selector error until the paired _SOURCES entry lands. Wanted diff (apply alongside this row): add 'telemetry-ledger': frozenset({..., "mcp-validation-round-trips-per-feature"}) to kpi-scorecard.py's existing 'telemetry-ledger' _SOURCES set (do NOT create a second 'telemetry-ledger' key), following the canary-trip-precision precedent (enum registered ahead of computation, NO-DATA until wired). Computation (not yet implemented): count blocker_kind: mcp-validation BLOCKED.md mint events (kind: 'halt' telemetry entries whose dispatched terminal_reason == 'blocked' and whose recorded blocker_kind == 'mcp-validation', keyed by item_id) per feature id in the window; renders NO-DATA until that reader is added to pipeline_visualizer.trends (mirroring halt_dwell/cycles_per_completion's read-the-ledger-never-reinfer discipline).

kpis.title: Design-gate scoped-change hit rate
kpis.friction: A rising share of scoped changes tripping the checker signals either genuine risk concentration or false-positive creep -- paired with the false-positive-rate row to disambiguate; tuning the numeric-literal detector to reduce it is itself a sign-off-gated weakening.
kpis.notes: Drafted in anti-overfit-design-gate SPEC.md's KPI Declaration; inserted at the STATE-lane ship-seam application (seam-deferred D3, this repo's park-provisional protocol -- the feature itself stays unratified/structurally-provisional, see docs/features/anti-overfit-design-gate/NEEDS_INPUT_PROVISIONAL.md). The harness-gate signal source + this selector are already registered in kpi-scorecard.py's _SOURCES (canary-trip-precision precedent -- enum ahead of computation); NO compute is wired here yet, so this row renders NO-DATA honestly until a harness-gate collector is built. Ratification of the feature can revert this row cleanly (delete it; no downstream coupling).

kpis.title: Gate-weakening override rate
kpis.friction: Operator sign-offs divided by gate-weakening hits -- a high rate means the gate is routinely asked to bless weakenings rather than fixing the underlying defect (Prohibition #2 erosion).
kpis.notes: Drafted in anti-overfit-design-gate SPEC.md's KPI Declaration; see the anti-overfit-gate-hit-rate row's notes for the shared seam-deferred/ratification-reversible provenance.

kpis.title: Design-gate false-positive burden
kpis.friction: Flags the verdict judged spurious over total flags -- the row that surfaces the numeric-literal-change detector's tuning debt (RESEARCH pitfall #1); a persistently high burden is the gate becoming friction.
kpis.notes: Drafted in anti-overfit-design-gate SPEC.md's KPI Declaration; see the anti-overfit-gate-hit-rate row's notes for the shared seam-deferred/ratification-reversible provenance.

kpis.title: Verdict-vs-efficacy disagreement
kpis.friction: Changes the gate PASSED that intervention-efficacy-tracking later REFUTED, plus changes it FLAGGED that were CONFIRMED -- the D6 self-audit signal that makes the gate falsifiable by data it does not control (a mis-tuned gate indicts itself here).
kpis.notes: Drafted in anti-overfit-design-gate SPEC.md's KPI Declaration; see the anti-overfit-gate-hit-rate row's notes for the shared seam-deferred/ratification-reversible provenance.
